name: Training Sword
description: A basic training sword, with a few notches and grooves from use.

name: 2Training Sword2
description: A basic training sword, with a few notches and grooves from use.

name: 3Training Sword3
description: A basic training sword, with a few notches and grooves from use.

name: Cooking Knife
description: A simple knife that may have been intended for food preparation, but will serve you nonetheless.

name: Dented Wooden Buckler
description: A worn down shield, likely constructed from the lid of a barrel, that likely won't survive much abuse.
effects: Light Armor: +0.2% Evasion

name: Notched Spear
description: You've heard the saying 'Another notch in your belt', but never suspected that someone might actually take it literally.... with a spear....

name: Spare Pitchfork
description: Every good farmhand knows the value of having a spare pitchfork on hand.

name: Lesser Ent Club
description: While many weapons need to be refined and meticulously crafted, a large chunk of wood with a tapered side to function as a handle is largely what any good club needs. When it can come from magically-imbued, living wood as base material, then even better.
effects: Ent Smash (Club): 10% chance to proc 50 Nature damage; +15 damage per Lesser Ent Wood set item worn

name: Brittle Cattle Whip
description: Having a tool capable of gaining compliance over thick-hided cows is essential to cattle-farming. Now you're simply turning it on thinner-skinned targets to gain a different kind of compliance...

name: Rusted Hewing Axe
description: While it is certainly preferable to keep your tools honed and well-cared for, occasionally these wisdoms go unheeded. Nevertheless, it seems well weighted, and still plenty sharp.

name: Entwood Staff
description: The same magics that imbued the docile Ents with life course through this staff, and they speak to the land as readily as they ever have. You can feel it coursing through the wood as you hold it, distinctly similar to that of your own pulse.
effects: Root Strike (Staff): 15% chance to proc 200 Nature damage; +5 Magic

name: Entwood Darts
description: While these darts appear solid and lacking any obvious opening, it is apparent from their weight that they must be hollow. Perhaps the insides contain the magical energies that occasionally cause roots to spring from the ground to lash out at their victims.
effects: Root Strike (Darts): 10% chance to proc 275 Nature damage; +5% Crit Damage

name: Entwood Shortbow
description: You were surprised to find a bow made from such stiff, unyielding wood, though you suspect that is why it was not crafted into a longbow. That said, despite your most vigorous uses of the weapon the wood refuses to splinter or twist, so perhaps the magic inherent in the wood has been put to good use.
effects: Root Strike (Shortbow): 5% chance to proc 500 Nature damage; +1% Crit Rate; +15% Crit Damage

name: Navenian Pavise
description: No-one seems able to provide a clear explanation for why this Pavise was found in such a small village as Naven, but despite how run-down its heavy frame looks it seems capable of withstanding significant force.
effects: Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Dented Wakizashi
description: A peculiar blade, notwithstanding its distinctive -and obviously unintended- dents, you feel more at-ease wielding this blade in your off-hand for some reason.

name: Gilded Crossbow
description: A beautifully crafted piece, this crossbow has gold and iron plating adoring it, improving the tension of the frame and allowing for more accurate - and powerful - shots.
effects: Truest Shot: 15% chance to proc 250 Physical damage; +1% Crit Rate; +3% Crit Damage

name: Thunderbrush
description: At first glance this seemed to be a regular spear, if bearing a number of intricate runes carved into its side. When Hamond clasped you on the shoulder from behind and was knocked prone from the ensuing flash of lightning is when you realized there was more to this.
effects: Thunder Fury: 20% chance to proc 200 Lightning damage; +3% Crit Damage

name: Watcher's Slitted Orb
description:
"Why would this orb have this thin, slitted section to it?"

Alanis rolls her eyes, and then they widen as she turns to look at you.

"How about you watch where you're pointing that slit before you ask any more foolish questions?"
effects: All-Seeing Blindness (Orb): 5% chance to proc 300 Dark damage; +30 damage per Watcher's set item worn

name: Watcher's Piercer
description: You swear Alanis nearly had a stroke when she saw you trying to fit this piercer into the Slitted Orb. Why else would they be shaped so perfectly if not to fit together?
effects: All-Seeing Blindness (Piercer): 5% chance to proc 300 Dark damage; +30 damage per Watcher's set item worn

name: Miray's Lance
description: I. The scent of dust and dried blood woke Miray as it had a thousand thousand times before, and she squinted as a ray of bright light shone through the small, barred window of her room. There was nothing remarkable about this day, and yet Miray knew it was to be the most remarkable day of her existence thus far. Sitting up and dropping her legs off of the stone slab, Miray yawned, her mind slowly recognizing the gravity that rested upon this day as she wiped the sleep from her eyes. She heard the metallic clangs from the practice yard, and knew that it would soon be her time to warm her muscles… only it would be for the last time.
effects: +10 HP; +10 Vitality; +10 Energy

name: Miray's Aegis
description: II. Rising to her feet, Miray stretched briefly, her mind simultaneously shifting between focusing upon the imminent future and her prospective fate, until the clanging against the bars of her domicile alerted her that it was her chance to use the practice yard. Grabbing her rusted, bronzed helm, her notched short-sword and her dented buckler, she nodded to the guard as he unlocked her barred path. Squaring off against the weighted dummy, Miray went through her usual forms of striking and reassessing, and had to force herself to focus on the task at hand when all she wanted to do was imagine a future that was no longer a distant thought. Nor did she want to wear herself out, for today was to mark the match that would culminate in her one thousandth victory in the arena.
effects:
+10 HP; +10 Vitality; +10 Energy
Light Armor: +0.2% Evasion

name: Ichibi Flail
description: This one-pronged flail seems capable of controlling sudden bursts of wind. The black etchings on it seem to flow and ebb as you wield this dusky tool.
effects: One-tailed Air Bullet: 22% chance to proc 120 Nature damage

name: Nibi Flail
description: The two lashes of this almost-ethereal flail burn with an uncanny blue light. The magical energies blasting from it cast an eerie, flaming light.
effects: Two-tailed Ethereal Flame: 16% chance to proc 200 Magic damage

name: Sanbi Flail
description: The solid, hard-as-stone weights at the end of the three tails of this flail are made of a material you cannot place. The ephermeral mist surrounding them as they strike only adds to the mystery.
effects: Three-tailed Turtleshell: 10% chance to proc 360 Physical damage

name: Yonbi Flail
description: The red tails of this flail seem to crack with unearthly volume when used. The flaming hunks of rock flung from it seem to elicit similar sounds from your target.
effects: Four-tailed Lava Slab: 5% chance to proc 800 Fire damage

name: Gobi Flail
description: The five strands of this flail seem to flow with impossible grace, never snapping against eachother as they move in a rhythmic dance. The steam produced by these movements seems to add impossible amounts of power to their blows.
effects: Five-tailed Steam Puncture: 20% chance to proc 320 Holy damage

name: Rokubi Flail
description: While the handle is firm in your grip, the tendril-like tails of this flail look distastefully squishy, and ooze a caustic, slime-like substance.
effects: Six-tailed Corrosive Slap: 40% chance to proc 250 Acid damage

name: Shichibi Flail
description: The whips at the end of this flail seem to flatten out, leaving an elegant and almost-delicate appearance. The glimmering dust they leave in their wake is quite disorienting.
effects: Seven-tailed Blinding Dust: 10% chance to proc 800 Psychic damage

name: Hachibi Flail
description: The eight violet tails appear almost as if they had come from a cephalopod. The ink they splatter everywhere only adds to this, and to the general mess.
effects: Eight-tailed Octo-Inking: 24% chance to proc 760 Dark damage

name: Kyuubi Flail
description: The nine lashes on this flail seem incapable of ever remaining fully still, and generate problematic amounts of static even when not in use. The blasts they send forth in battle, however, bring to mind natural disasters.
effects: Nine-tailed Storm Blast: 33% chance to proc 800 Lightning damage

name: Serpentine Darts
description: These darts have been dipped in the venom of the Risaldar, and seem capable of bringing down a man in a few minutes, if left unchecked.
effects: +5% Crit Damage; Ssstrike!: 12% chance to proc 80 Poison damage

name: Legacy Greatshield
description: Bearing an unusual engraving along the inner-looped clasp, you recognize it from years earlier, undeniably having belonged to your father. How the Naga came to possess it, however, is something you can only hope to one-day learn.

name: Naga Neend Darts
description: Containing a strange toxin within them, these darts are capable of causing their victims to see strange, hypnotic patterns, often collapsing due to the effects of the trance, only to groggily waken several moments later.
effects: Toxic Torpor: 4% chance to proc 750 Poison damage and reduce the target's damage to both Player and Units to 0 for their retaliation attack; +25% Crit Damage

name: Hooded Veil Stabber
description:
I. "Long again, a priesstesss of our order rose up, not in honour of Ssarfraxss but in opposition to him, and while sshe wass unpopular amongsst many, sshe quickly gained a ssignificant following who ssought optionss beyond brute force and mayhem"
-Seksehsa
effects: Fangs Alot (Stabber): 16% chance to proc 250 Poison damage; +25 damage per Hooded Veil set item worn; +6 Offence

name: Hooded Veil Slicer
description:
II. "It iss ssaid that sshe wass immenssely powerful, but rather than jusst employing potent venomss sshe masstered various divine artss, though the namess of her patronss wass never sshared."
-Seksehsa
effects: Fangs Alot (Slicer): 18% chance to proc 200 Poison damage; +25 damage per Hooded Veil set item worn; +6 Offence

name: Saber of Bloody Freedom
description:
"My great grandfather often said, when he saw any of his kin hesitating, 'If you feel fear, refrain from action, but once taking action you have nothing to fear.'"
-Borjigin Kuyuk
effects: One Man's Saviour: 8% chance to proc 900 Dark damage; +250 damage if Jida of Sovereign Hope is worn; +100 damage per Conqueror's set item worn

name: Jida of Sovereign Hope
description: Like a plague they spread across the Dolmtaalian continent, conquering through unrelenting bloodshed and terror. Only upon this massacre could the now-bloodsoaked land fertilize new hope, and that hope grew more vigourously than ever imagined. All of this, courtesy the military efforts of the Warlord Temujin.
effects: Another Man's Villain: 12% chance to proc 800 Holy damage; +200 damage if Saber of Bloody Freedom is worn; +100 damage per Conqueror's set item worn

name: Marksman's Longbow
description:
"The careful eye of a marksman - or a markswoman - is only the second most important feature they possess. Their steady frame and grip of their tools make a far greater difference, particularly when the most subtle of distances matter most."
-Yathana Shahara
effects: Ranger's Eye: 24% chance to proc 45 Physical damage; +1% Crit Rate; +5% Crit Damage

name: Sung Wooden Boomerang
description: I. The history behind Singing material long predates even the oldest living elves. It is said that there was a race of forest people, whose name has been lost to time, who first discovered the art while Singing to their trees.
effects: Sungwood Symphony (Boomerang): 10% chance to proc 230 Physical damage; 5% chance to proc 200 Physical damage; +50 damage [each] per Sung Leather set item worn

name: Sungreed Bullwhip
description: II. The Songs were said to be wordless, but rather were deep, thrumming tones, simultaneously overlaid with baritone and soprano pitches in their mellifluous ballads.
effects: Sungwood Symphony (Bullwhip): 16% chance to proc 180 Physical damage; +50 damage per Sung Leather set item worn

name: Obsidian Rod
description:
"Even in the pitch stones from the darkest tunnels will the light of Orimneni shine. For it is not proximity to the Gods that matters most, but rather our faith in their presence that shines most brilliantly."

-Dondomra Grimfeet
effects: Refractory Lumen: 12% chance to proc 600 Holy damage; +600 damage vs Underground raids

name: Granite-Blesser's Rod
description: I. The Granite-Blessers are one of the larger followings dedicated to Orimneni, but may well be the most well-known outside of purely dwarven circles. According to Dondomra, this is largely due to the missionary work accomplished by the group extending beyond the halls of the dwarves, and offering their services to a variety of people.
effects: Granite's Greatness (Rod): 14% chance to proc 225 Holy damage; +60 damage per Granite-Blesser's set item worn

name: Granite-Blesser's Guard
description: II. Many aeons past, Orimneni took it upon herself to breath holy life into the rock, and the dwarves have striven to uncover and understand her work ever since. There are philosophical debates as to whether Orimneni was the one to construct the dwarven people and breathe life into them, or whether Skirfmir first constructed them and Orimneni gave them life, but fortunately these disagreements rarely lead to deadly confrontations, anymore.
effects:
Granite's Greatness (Guard): 14% chance to proc 225 Holy damage; +60 damage per Granite-Blesser's set item worn
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Arachnean Eye
description: Legends surrounding Rhacel are a strange lot, for while they are plentiful and varied, there are few who can speak to the sources of the tales. One thing that is clear, however, is that the true followers of Rhacel have little consideration for those who stand in the way of their mistress' schemes.
effects: Compounding Distortion (Eye): 4% chance to proc 1245 Psychic damage; +165 damage per Arachnean set item worn

name: Arachnean Leg Segments
description: The ideals of Rhacel may seem perverse to many, but there is no questioning the efficacy of their methodology. They often lead to destruction and terror, but through the path requiring the least effort.
effects: Compounding Distortion (Leg Segments): 4% chance to proc 1245 Psychic damage; +165 damage per Arachnean set item worn

name: Dokumotsu Crossbow
description:
"Cave spiders are not an unusual sight, and while most rely on intricate webs to ensnare their fare, it is often accompanied by potent venoms. Some have even described these excretions as caustic, capable burning through flesh and bone alike."
-Norsic Bittermail
effects: Multipod Spray (Crossbow): 12% chance to proc 140 Acid damage; +40 damage per Dokumotsu set item worn; +2.5% Crit Rate; +16% Crit Damage

name: Aragnes' Guard
description: Said to have been wielded by the most devout acolyte of Rhacel, Aragnes was not content with merely defending her patroness' name, but only in spreading Her potent venoms would She be satisfied.
effects: Spindleslam: 32% chance to proc 320 Poison damage; +20% Poison Resistance

name: Ranging Scout's Longbow
description:
"The key to using a longbow is, predictably, your eyes. However, to truly master it, you have to see not only where your target is, but where they are going to be."

You nod your head, unsure if the Marksman has the gift of prophetic vision, or is pulling your leg.
effects: Scout's Honor (Longbow): 16% chance to proc 75 Physical damage; +25 damage per Ranging Scout's set item worn; +1% Crit Rate; +5% Crit Damage

name: Honorbound's Dagger
description:
"You are correct that knives are not typical dwarven weapons, Farmer. The curved dagger the Honorbound are given represents the wounds inflicted upon their people, and the feelings of being cut deeply by such horrific actions."
-Norsic Bittermail
effects: +2% Crit Rate

name: Honorbound's Maul
description:
"The weight of the hammer, while certainly more suitable a tool for most dwarves, reflects the weight of their transgressions against their people. That it is wielded in the weaker hand is seen as an insult, and adds to the shame the Honorbound must endure as a part of their penance."
-Norsic Bittermail
effects: +20% Crit Damage

name: Pentwater Mace
description: I. Dwarven legend, passed down by word of mouth, tells of a terrible uprising that threatened to wipe the dwarves from existence. Countless hordes of demons, sent by Malbog, raged a violent war for the underbelly of the land as the Demon Lord sought to gain a foothold on this plane. One heroic dwarf is credited with ultimately staving off these forces and driving them back.
effects:
Blessed Boulder-Be (Mace): 1% chance to proc 1000 Holy damage; +200 damage per Pentwater set item worn
Consecrated

name: Pentwater's Stand
description: II. The dwarven hero, Malaclaven Pentwater, was a devout individual, having traveled in his youth before studying under the tutelage of various scholars, ultimately dedicating himself to the teachings of Chaunath. He wielded a great shield, as was Her sigil, and bore down divine energies in massive bursts to strike out at his blasphemous foes.
effects:
Blessed Boulder-Be (Stand): 50% chance to proc 15 Holy damage; +4 damage per Pentwater set item worn
Sanctified
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Firedrake's Wand
description: I. Long before the first Dragon War, a young mage sought to make a name for herself, but while she had a keen sense for mystical engineering, she had a decidedly lacking sense of innovation and creativity.
effects:
Drake's Embers (Wand): 25% chance to proc 60 Fire damage; +8 damage per Firedrake set item worn
Set Bonus (Firedrake's Set) for wearing the stated number of equipment pieces from this set:
 7+: +5% Fire Resistance

name: Firedrake's Glass Eye
description: II. Not particularly skilled in the elemental arts, Nura was gifted in imbuement, and capable of turning even the most mundane items into powerful artifacts, given the right materials.
effects:
Drake's Embers (Glass Eye): 50% chance to proc 28 Fire damage; +4 damage per Firedrake set item worn
Set Bonus (Firedrake's Set) for wearing the stated number of equipment pieces from this set:
 7+: +5% Fire Resistance

name: Red Dragonhunter's Lance
description: I. While the stories tell little of where he came from, they are plentiful about the hero Qusasper who, amongst many notable deeds, was said to have slain a dragon in its own fire.
effects: Red Drake Stalker (Lance): 12% chance to proc 400 Ice damage; +800 damage vs Dragon raids; +40 damage per Red Dragonhunter set item worn

name: Red Dragonhunter's Shield
description: II. While traveling abroad, Qusasper was said to have stopped on a hill for his afternoon tea, but the boiling waters wakened a great, red dragon that was slumbering nearby.
effects: Red Drake Stalker (Shield): 20% chance to proc 150 Ice damage; +400 damage vs Dragon raids; +40 damage per Red Dragonhunter set item worn

name: Blade of Mestari
description: Said to be a gift from the Gods themselves, this blade shimmers with divine radiance and feels unusually light in your hand. It was said to have been lost to time, but in reforging it you feel as though it has retained its holy essence.
effects: Divine Empowerment: 40% chance to proc 150 Holy damage

name: Rattler Club
description: There is a certain irony to using the bones of your foe to enhance your magics, and an even greater one in using them to cave in another foe's skull.
effects: Shocking Shaker: 7% chance to proc 700 Lightning damage; +20 Magic

name: Serpent's Staff
description: Channeling the powers bestowed by Ssarfraxss, this weapon is prone to wild outbursts of violent, and perhaps venomous, magics.
effects: Naga Channeling: 12% chance to proc 400 Magic damage; +15 Magic

name: Cumberworld Poppers
description:
"It's fun and games for all who came, and woe for the foe who decided to show!"
-Bonaguido
effects:
+20 Vitality; +20 Energy
Set Bonus (Cumberworld Set) for wearing the stated number of equipment pieces from this set:
 5+: +1 Tiers of 'Discerning Vision' and 'Astute Observation'
 9+: +1 Tiers of 'Discerning Vision' and 'Astute Observation'

name: Cumberworld Paddle
description:
"With a crack and a smack, you lay the target upon their back, or use it as an oar and sail back for more."
-Bonaguido
effects:
+20 Vitality; +20 Energy
Set Bonus (Cumberworld Set) for wearing the stated number of equipment pieces from this set:
 5+: +1 Tiers of 'Discerning Vision' and 'Astute Observation'
 9+: +1 Tiers of 'Discerning Vision' and 'Astute Observation'

name: Scolfield's Lash
description:
"Discipline is vital when serving the House Scolfield, and if it isn't inherent in her employees you can be certain they will be taught the true meaning of the word."
-Scolfield Retainer
effects:
+30 Vitality; +30 Energy; +30 Honor
Set Bonus (Scolfield Set) for wearing the stated number of equipment pieces from this set:
 5+: +1 Tiers of 'Discerning Vision', 'Astute Observation' and 'Precise Inspection'
 9+: +1 Tiers of 'Discerning Vision', 'Astute Observation' and 'Precise Inspection'

name: Scolfield's Wand
description:
"While she may not look it, the Lady Scolfield is an accomplished mage, and expertly skilled in various mystic arts. It's nigh-impossible to truly catch her off-guard."
-Scolfield Retainer
effects:
+30 Vitality; +30 Energy; +30 Honor
Set Bonus (Scolfield Set) for wearing the stated number of equipment pieces from this set:
 5+: +1 Tiers of 'Discerning Vision', 'Astute Observation' and 'Precise Inspection'
 9+: +1 Tiers of 'Discerning Vision', 'Astute Observation' and 'Precise Inspection'

name: Thunderbird's Rod
description:
"The Thunderbird, despite its name, is actually wholly unable to create proper claps of thunder. Blasts of lightning-fury, however, is entirely expected."
-Janagud
effects: Graced by Thunder (Rod): 24% chance to proc 500 Lightning damage; +25 damage per Thunderbird set item worn

name: Thunderbird's Swordbreaker
description:
"Graceful in flight, the Thunderbird is equally likely to evade a battle as to seek one out, and will elegantly feast upon the charred flesh of those it fells."
-Janagud
effects: Graced by Thunder (Swordbreaker): 24% chance to proc 500 Lightning damage; +25 damage per Thunderbird set item worn

name: Meftet's Blade
description:
"The blade you carry seems to be an homage to the myths surrounding the Meftet, suggesting they will guide the unwary to the underworld, or are beasts that dwell therein."
-Janagud
effects: Capital Justice (Blade): 30% chance to proc 400 Dark damage; +20 damage per Meftet set item worn

name: Meftet's Plumbatae
description:
"The claws of the Meftet are said to be more akin to needles than knives, and are hidden until the moment they lash out with them. Those darts offer a fair comparison, I'd say."
-Janagud
effects: Capital Justice (Plumbatae): 30% chance to proc 400 Dark damage; +20 damage per Meftet set item worn

name: Zaratan's Mace
description:
"The impact force of being struck by a Zaratan is perhaps one of the most devastating found in nature. However, in my experience, the beasts are incredibly gentle, and rarely prone to attack, even when provoked."
-Janagud
effects: Debris Volley (Mace): 15% chance to proc 800 Nature damage; +40 damage per Zaratan set item worn

name: Zaratan's Guard
description:
"It has been said that the defence of the Zaratan is utterly impregnable. Given that you have that shield, this could not be the case."
-Janagud
effects: Debris Volley (Guard): 15% chance to proc 800 Nature damage; +40 damage per Zaratan set item worn

name: Stipelash
description: While it isn't uncommon to use a whip made of natural materials, you were previously only familiar with those derived from either plant or animal origins....
effects: Fluorishing Flail: 30% chance to proc 160 Nature damage

name: Giant Toadstool Cap
description: Spongy though this may feel, this strange cap seems surprisingly capable at absorbing impacts directed at you. You would prefer if it didn't release strange spores upon such impacts, however...
effects:
Spores-Galore: 24% chance to proc 180 Poison damage; +10% Poison Resistance
Light Armor: +0.2% Evasion

name: Raid Crossbow
description:
"Ah, yes, that weapon should fare well in annihilating the arthropodic menace; it'll kill the bug right dead."
-Ailmar Perjeon
effects: Allethrin Assault: 12% chance to proc 90 Acid damage; +1% Crit Rate; +2% Crit Damage

name: Melf's Sprig
description: Legends say that Melf was one of the most imaginative and potent wizards of his time... but even though it took genius to invent new tools as an infant, it's arguable how worthwhile such creations truly were...
effects: Acid Spittle: 30% chance to proc 50 Acid damage

name: Capranidas' Hammer
description: The warrior-king Capranidas was renowned for his honour, and it is said that even the greatest attempts to besmirch that honour only added to it. It seems he was such an honourable leader that it even rubbed off upon his arms.
effects: Force of a Nation: 16% chance to proc 500 Physical damage; +20 Honor

name: Semliki Spear
description:
"This kind spear, you no throw. Stab, hard. Fast. No let go."
-Dekoo Harpooner

name: Katanda Harpoon
description:
"This harpoon. Much harder use. Barbs unforgiving. Throw far, but never let go of rope. No tie rope to arm, or lose arm too."
-Dekoo Harpooner
effects: Barbed Barbarism: 6% chance to proc 3000 Dark damage vs Aquatic raids; +2% Crit Rate

name: Weighted Net
description:
"It may seem barbaric, at first, weighing down your prey with these nets. Just remember, they live by the sea and oft not die from the sea. It certainly makes harpooning them all the easier, though!"
-Rokovok Harpooner

name: Barbed Rete
description:
"The barbs on this rete are far less forgiving than mere weights, but it takes considerably more skill to wield such a tool. It is far more punishing for a target to try to evade or remove than weights, as well!"
-Rokovok Harpooner
effects: Heartless Hooks: 40% chance to proc 200 Dark damage vs Aquatic raids

name: Stevedore's Dirk
description: While somewhat larger than what you would expect a dockhand to be carrying, the stevedores seem quite adept at handling their blades as multi-purpose tools.
effects:
Pier Puncture: 26% chance to proc 220 Physical damage
Set Bonus (Stevedore's Set) for wearing the stated number of equipment pieces from this set:
 6+: +5% All Resistances
 8+: +5% All Resistances

name: Stevedore's Cudgel
description: You wouldn't have expected the seemingly-soft leather cudgels wielded by the stevedores to be particularly capable.... until you met a would-be thief with a jaw that clearly did not align properly.
effects:
Wharf Walloping: 12% chance to proc 450 Physical damage
Set Bonus (Stevedore's Set) for wearing the stated number of equipment pieces from this set:
 6+: +5% All Resistances
 8+: +5% All Resistances

name: Duty Collector's Orb
description:
"With a round orb like that, aren't you worried about dropping it, and it rolling into the sea?"

"If I was prone to dropping this orb, I would not have deserved to possess it in the first place."
-Conversations with a Wharf Duty Collector
effects: Duties Owed (Orb): 12% chance to proc 240 Psychic damage; +80 Psychic damage per Duty Collector's set item worn; +40 Magic

name: Duty Collector's Shield
description:
"Surely there is no need to walk around with a shield of that size! Are you expecting to block cannon fire?!"

"If you've spent any time on these docks, adventurer, you'd know there are far worse things to fear than mere cannons."
-Conversations with a Wharf Duty Collector
effects:
Duties Owed (Shield): 22% chance to proc 140 Psychic damage
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Mirror Shield
description: As beneficial as this reflective service may be in particular instances - including preening over your overborn, ugly visage, according to Alanis - you are exceedingly concerned about using it to turn a blow from a sword, or mace.
effects:
Divine Reflection: 5% chance to proc 300 Holy damage vs Beast raids
Light Armor: +0.2% Evasion

name: Capon Cane
description: Perhaps the shortest staff you have ever seen, the magical powers within this wooden stave cannot be discounted. Perhaps some mage appreciated the value in pairing practicality with the arcane.
effects: Cockerel's Crowing: 40% chance to proc 140 Psychic damage

name: Rapier of Swift-Cleaving
description: While blades meant for adventuring or dueling are left thicker to avoid shattering, those intended for executions are honed to the keenest of edges. Being able to cleave the target's head from their body in a swift stroke is vital.
effects: Beheading Blow: 10% chance to proc 700 Physical damage; +2% Crit Rate

name: Lance's Spear
description: You wouldn't have thought much of this particular spear, except for a nigh-illegible scribble on the side of the shaft reading as "Propurtee uv lants".
effects: Tip of the Day: +1% Crit Rate; +2% Crit Damage

name: Dilay's Dirk
description: This small blade appears nondescript at first glance, but a careful inspection of the hilt reveals the mechanism where stored poisons can be released onto the dirk.
effects: Foul Edge: 10% chance to proc 160 Poison damage

name: Derangement Darts
description: The resourceful mercenary plans for the unexpected, and knows that not every raid will go as they expect. Having a backup plan, and the tools to carry it out (even when it involves backing out entirely) is a necessity in that line of work.
effects: Destructive Distraction: 100% chance to reduce incoming damage to the Player by 20% during this attack

name: Wand of Fire Blast
description:
"Not every magical artifact possesses a wide array of magical properties. Some can simply enhance the wielder's latent powers, while others may only cast one single spell - though likely to great effectiveness."
-Alanis
effects: Explosive Expectation: 2% chance to proc 3600 Fire damage; +10 Magic

name: Shale Recurve Bow

description: VI. Approaching the final stretch, passing by a mountain crest alongside a ravine, Bevis lacked the motivation to do much besides stay beside the wagon... up until they heard the unearthly howls fill the evening air. Easily distinguishable from wolves, Bevis and his father exchanged looks at a distance before his father doubled the pace of the flock in order to hurry to open ground. However, whether catalyzed by the howls or the sudden movement, the mountain had decided to unload its contents and a rockslide began to fall towards the flock.
effects: Bulwark's Blow: 18% chance to proc 166 Physical damage

name: Bevis' Training Shield
description: VII. Acting without thought Bevis tore off the cover-rimmed wheel from the wagon and rushed up to the highest point of the path, planting his feet and facing the oncoming deluge of stone and earth despite his father crying out for him to get to safety. As the rocks poured down Bevis' father expected the worst... up until the whole avalanche seemed to inexplicably stop entirely. Seemingly beyond comprehension, Bevis was holding back the largest of the stones, bracing his makeshift shield against them, and it took his father a moment to realize that Bevis was calling out for him to hurry the flock across to safety. After several long moments Bevis released the rocks and ran back, rejoining his father and the flock, leaving only a semi-supported wagon and broken wheel to be crushed beneath the flow.

name: Mephitic Chiffon Staff
description: VIII. Brath departed early the next day, deciding to forgo further information gathering in Rispa and head for the Lenha Woods. The trip was relatively smooth, though he had forgotten how much he disliked walking long distances, but while a sense of familiarity overcame him when, on the third day, the edge of the forest came into view, he felt a tinge of fear and worry alongside it. He had grown up not too far from here, near the Toisar Forest, but despite the similarities in terrain and flora Brath could sense an unsettled quiet in the land. Camping just inside the edge of the woods that eve, Brath took some time to meditate and commune with the land, feeling the energy of the land flow through him, drawing more in with every breath. The land was truly shaken, and suffering a sense of dire confusion that Brath could not clearly recognize, though he was certain of one thing: dark, druidic powers were being channeled here, and on an incredible scope.
effects:
Downy Destruction (Staff): 40% chance to proc 375 Holy damage; +6,250 damage vs Guild Raids; +125 damage per Mephitic Chiffon set item worn; 25% chance to proc 400 Poison damage; +150 damage per Mephitic Chiffon set item worn; 2% chance to heal the Player 6 HP
Set Bonus (Mephitic Chiffon Set) for wearing the stated number of equipment pieces from this set:
 9+: +6% All Resistances; 1% chance to heal the Player 5 HP

name: Mephitic Chiffon Buckler Shield
description: IX. Listening to the earth, the air and the plants provided Brath with a relatively clear path to follow in order to identify the source of the corruption, despite the shadows of the trees reaching far as the evening sky darkened. It wasn't too long before the sounds Brath could hear shifted from the mystic and natural to that of voices, and Brath paused to listen. The chanting sounded to be an old dialect that Brath could not truly make out, but it sounded rehearsed rather than fluent, so that provided some small comfort. Brath crept forward, recognizing that he was far from skilled at stealth, but as he did he uttered a quiet chant to seek power from the nearby shrubs in order to further mask his approach. The spell worked, but Brath was aghast to realize that the shrubs he had sought assistance from died from the draw of power, small as it was; they were already being strained to their limit from the magics the cult was channeling, and had apparently been so for some time.
effects:
Downy Destruction (Buckler Shield): 40% chance to proc 375 Holy damage; +6,250 damage vs Guild Raids; +125 damage per Mephitic Chiffon set item worn; 25% chance to proc 400 Poison damage; +150 damage per Mephitic Chiffon set item worn

 9+: +6% All Resistances; 1% chance to heal the Player 5 HP
Light Armor: +0.2% Evasion

name: Mordant Moswa's Wand
description: I. "But even you can see how swapping the natural element for these reagents would improve the yield severalfold... Sir." Brath Hartwood closed his eyes and inhaled deeply, wondering why he had agreed to such an arrangement as teaching, of all things, and paused before turning to face the second-year acolyte. "The yield was never in question... the issue is, and always was, the byproducts of the reaction. Which, if you'd done the reading I had assigned, you'd know are exceedingly volatile. So, through your 'improved' efforts to enhance the capacity for sustainable agriculture in urban environments you'd be more like than not to level a city block/." Brath's calm tone did little to brace the student from the weight of his words, and Brath noticed one of the first-year acolytes in the corner - Luciani, he believed - chuckling quietly at the retort.
effects: Acidic Antlers (Wand): 25% chance to proc 2,080 Magic damage; +10,000 damage vs Guild Raids; +320 damage per Mordant Moswa set item worn; 6% chance to proc 6000 Nature damage; +1000 damage per Mordant Moswa set item worn; 25% chance to proc 400 Acid damage; +150 damage per Mordant Moswa set item worn; +400 Magic

name: Mordant Moswa's Orb
description: II. Brath watched passively as the student glowered before stalking away, and it took effort for Brath to return to his small office before collapsing into his chair. For all of his experience and studies, and all of his talents in the arcane arts, Brath recognized that he was still unable to overcome a simple truth of the world: Basic economics. Protecting the arcane energies in those forests that still possessed them, and attempting to spread the potency of the earth was all well and good, but researching how to do so further, faster and more effectively took funding, and for that reason he had agreed to take on this teaching position at the Sunbringer Institute. Fortunately - or unfortunately, depending upon the day - he only had to suffer through the secondary acolytes, and didn't have to train the newest initiates, but that often came with increased ego and willingness to balk at his lessons.

name: Hatuumin Bare Whip
description: I(a). Sinyu grimaced as dark clouds covered the sky, promising to bring severe winds that would surely lead to terrible sandstorms. He felt a tug at his hand, and he squeezed the young boy's hand in consolation as he led him down the back alley. This mission had been given to him by the Nomarch, himself, and this darkness would aid his escape to the north, bringing with him the Nomarch's only son. A job that could only be trusted to a strong, military-minded commander, he was told, though he feared that there was something else afoot.

name: Hatuumin Mace
description: II(a). Several weeks later, after traveling to Yubqal, at the point of contemplating his return to Agiz, Sinyu heard the locals talking about the deposed Nomarch. Frantic to learn more, Sinyu demanded what details he could wring from anyone he could speak with, but there were precious few details: The Nomarch was gone, presumably killed, and a new line of royalty had been established. Sinyu wanted to weep, but turned to seek out his young ward, still unsure of what his next steps would be.

name: Hatuumin Points
description:
I(c). Kontar grimaced as he looked up into the ever-present sun, before looking back down to the street below and throwing the large stone he held. He dropped to his stomach as he did so, feeling the warmth of the stone roof under him as he heard a boy's voice call out in pain, then anger. He suspected he would pay a sharp price for the jest once he returned to the Alaytam, and no doubt the Denmother would see through his feigned innocence, but it was worth it in his eyes. He had little else to do, these days, especially when it wasn't a day he was scheduled to assist the old mage-

Kontar paused as he heard a blast, followed by a scream, a block or two away. Knowing, or at least fearing he knew where it originated, Kontar took off running towards the residence, wondering what he would find.
effects:

name: Hatuumin Shooter
description:
II(c). The scene was beyond description, and Kontar rushed through the decimated quarters, where he found his master in front of a body. Calling out to him, Kontar was able to usher him outside, seeing the man's shock turn to grief and disbelief as he did so.

"There must be a way... I... I can fix this!" Sennie was gasping for air, before he looked down at his palms, and saw the dark stars spreading outwards from under his skin. Seeing the obvious signs of the curse in his master, Kontar realized that he couldn't leave him alone, nor could he do this alone. He would have to call on an old friend in order to see these wrongs righted.

name: Hatuumin Sprig
description: I(b). Sennie sighed, putting his hand on the large, bound book on his table for the dozenth time, before removing it. All his years of study had culminated in this latest expedition, and the prize of his efforts had been this dark tome... That he was thrice-damned for taking, and equally so for fearing to use. He considered calling in his assistant, if he could be considered such, and as his summoning spell was coalescing he decided against it, and let it drop. He had to be decisive, and all of his work, all of his ambition would be for nothing if he couldn't confirm his research. He grabbed the book once more, and opened it in one swift motion.

name: Hatuumin Baton
description: II(b). As light slowly crept into his blurred field of vision, Sennie struggled to pull himself to his feet. His study was in shambles... until he realized he was not in his study. He looked around, barely able to ascertain that he was in his kitchen, and having no concept of why he was - Aya! Sennie surged to his feet, using the walls to balance himself as he made his way to his bedroom, only to see his wife - or what was left of her - dismembered upon the ground, clutching the silent body of their daughter. He was unable to process what he was seeing, and it took several moments before he heard his name, and looked over to see his assistant, clutching him by the forearm, pulling him out of the small residence.

name: Hatuumin Whip
description:
X(a). Sinyu paused and inadvertently allowed himself a moment to marvel at the strange landscape, adorned with colours and life, before turning back to the task at hand. Looking forward, he saw several more undead creatures rising from the ground. Glancing back to see how his support was looking, Sinyu cursed when he realized that both Kontar and Sennie were missing. He knew Kontar wouldn't have run off, and while it was possible that Sennie had pulled some strange stunt, his whip and mace still appeared to be enchanted, suggesting he hadn't been abandoned for dead. Sinyu leaped into the fray, realizing the enchantments were waning as combat dragged on, but the sad look in the eyes of these newer creatures tugged at him, even as he struck them down.

'I know this man!' Sinyu thought to himself, or at least he had known him, as he took an arm off another blighted creature. Peering about in a daze, Sinyu realized he was surrounded, but that each of the beings facing him were men he had led into combat, and who had perished under his command.

name: Hatuumin Mace-Axe
description: XI(a). As the last of the enchantments faded from Sinyu's weapons, he lunged forward, kicking the nearest creature back, knowing that he could not stop them any longer. He regretted losing sight of Kontar, and hoped that he would be okay, and he muttered a silent prayer for his wife and son, wishing he could have said goodbye. Sinyu stood up, looking the nearest creature in its eyes as it lumbered towards him, and bowed his head, accepting his fate. He had driven these men to their demise, and it was only fitting that they bring about his own. Sinyu closed his eyes, waiting for the final blows to reign upon him... Except they didn't. Looking up in bewilderment, Sinyu could only gape when he saw the creatures kneeling before him in supplication. Somehow, Sinyu knew that they had forgiven him whatever guilt he felt he owed them, and as he saw the portal open up before him, he knew he had accomplished what he could, here.

name: Hatuumin Darts
description:
X(c). Kontar stumbled forward, unprepared for the short drop in his step, before looking up and gaping with wonder. The world was scintillating and colourful, with all types of different plant and colour flooding his vision in every direction. Unable to help himself, he laughed aloud and rushed forward, taking in the sights, warmth and smells. This was a world completely foreign to him, and yet, so filled with... Where did Sinyu go?

The realization hit Kontar like a fist, and he noticed that he could not find either of his companions. Unperturbed, Kontar started looking around for them, certain he could find them sooner or later, and this land was certainly a marvel to explore in the meantime.

Into his third hour, however Kontar's anxiety was skyrocketing. Despite all the wonder of this realm, he was unable to find either Sennie or Sinyu, or ANYONE else, for that matter. For perhaps the first time in his life, he was truly alone.
effects:

name: Hatuumin Crossbow
description:
XI(c). Feeling a sense of urgency and panic, Kontar dashed forward, scaling the antiquated structures to get a better view of the landscape, but his fears only grew as the wider view remained equally empty. Climbing back to the ground, Kontar grasped at the sides of his head, his vision blurring and closing in as his breathing hastened. As he could hear his heart racing in his ears, Kontar's vision started to blacken and panic overwhelmed him.

'Is it truly so bad, son?'

Kontar looked up in disbelief, startled by the voice he vaguely recognized, and he saw a shimmering, golden man in fancy, if comfortable, dressings.

'It's been a while, so-'

'Father!'

Kontar rushed forward to embrace the man he now recognized as he father, tears flowing from his eyes.

name: Hatuumin Wand
description: X(b). Looking around at the dark and forlorn landscape, Sennie recoiled as he saw hosts of undead creatures lumbering about, and prepared himself for another wave of combat. That alertness was soon replaced with the realization that the creatures here did not appear to take notice of him, let alone have any inclination to attack him. That realization was, in turn, replaced by repulsion, as he saw the decay and dismemberment in the deathly beings, and that was only exasperated by their stench. As Sennie wandered forward, beginning to wonder what power it would take to control such creatures, another realization suddenly hit him: Did he condemn his wife and daughter to this same fate?

name: Hatuumin Rod
description:
XI(b). Sennie's despair began to overwhelm him once more, and his vision blurred as his mind raced regarding the possible tortuous fates his family had been subjected to... All in the name of his own ambitions and thirst for power. Sennie was aghast in his actions, and his past motivations, and fell to his knees, weeping. There was nothing he could do to make this right, and despite having come so far, he would never receive absolution...

There was NOTHING he could do to make this right, and yet, just maybe, he could avoid repeating this same mistake, in the future. That glimmer of hope shone through his vision like a blinding light, and as his tears dried, he looked up, seeing his wife holding their young daughter, smiling with understanding, seeming to send her love through that one, short look. In another moment, she was gone.

name: Hatuumin Weighted Whip
description: XII(a). Stepping through the portal, Sinyu was met with the dark, stone dais he had first seen the portal upon. Looking around him, Sinyu noted that there was no sign of any portal at the moment. Sinyu walked over to a stone wall and leaned against it, deciding whether he should wait for the others or not. After some time passed, however, during which Sinyu digested what had transpired, he stood and started heading up the spiraling stone stairwell. The others would certainly have to overcome challenges of their own, and it was unlikely he would be seeing them any time soon. Sinyu felt a pang of sadness, leaving Kontar behind, but he had faith that the boy would be alright, and hoped that their paths would cross once more. In the meantime, Sinyu knew he had someone to go see.

name: Hatuumin Regal Mace-Axe
description:
XIII(a). Entering Agiz with no second glance from the guard, Sinyu breathed a silent sigh of relief as he quickly turned down a series of streets he recognized, barely noticing the changes to some of the building fronts from what his recollection recalled. Arriving at his intended goal, he paused, unsure whether to enter or knock on the door, and Sinyu finally decided to walk in and seek whatever fate awaited him.

"I'm home. At last."

Sinyu paused, before he heard a chopping knife cease, and saw a young woman come around the corner and gasp. He stepped forward, and she rushed to embrace him, and the moment was broken only by Sinyu noticing a boy, a few years younger than Kontar, peering around the wall to look up at him with uncertainty.

He was finally home, and he now knew that he would do whatever was necessary to protect that fact, right up to dealing with the threat still seated as Nomarch.

name: Hatuumin Sovereign Darts
description: XII(c). Kontar had only fragments of memories of his father speaking with him, almost a decade earlier, but as he sat on the stone, his father kneeling before him, he realized how much he valued this opportunity to share a conversation with the man. He could see that, despite having had access to everything the nation could offer, there was a deep loneliness within his father's eyes. In turn, his father shared his wisdom, including recognizing that these were valid feelings to have, but that he would never be entirely alone, for he had those who had come before him, and those who stood alongside him in support. Kontar was uncertain as to how long the two spoke, but once the conversation ended, Kontar wiped his cheeks, and walked back towards the now-visible portal, departing the blasted lands.

name: Hatuumin Arcanym
description: XII(b). Climbing to his feet, Sennie looked around, noticing that the landscape seemed much more brilliantly coloured than he had recalled, and while the denizens of this place continued to pass, they didn't appear as menacing as they once had; even the smell seemed to be pleasantly improving. Sennie noted that he could see tendrils of magic flowing out from each of the undead beings, and from various structures and plants surrounding him, but he turned about without a second thought. The hunt for this type of power is what had driven him to desperation, and had already cost him too much: He would not be tempted by such power again. Seeing the portal appear behind him, Sennie stepped through, and departed the cavern, feeling a strange sense of satiation.

Rather than searching for sources of power, including that from other realms and cursed artifacts, Sennie's studies focused upon understanding the world around him, and the magical properties to be found in the seemingly-mundane. Not quite ready to take on a new apprentice - and even less ready to seek out a new companion - Sennie paused and sighed... He knew he would have to give himself some more time, but also that he would be ready again, one day.

description: VIII. Word of Bevis’ strength in this exploit soon reached the ears of the regional lords, and one knight, whose name has long been lost in retellings, took particular interest in the tale. The knight, apparently known for an honourable forfeit against an injured opponent in a tournament’s joust, spent months tracking down the path the story took, before finally locating Bevis and his father. While the stories are sparse on the details of the exchange, Bevis agreed to join the knight, though refused to become his squire, in order to begin a regimented training that would further enhance his already-impressive strength.

name: Quilltip's Rod
description: VI. The crowd seemed frozen for a moment as Loric finished speaking, before launching into a series of questions, which Loric was capable of deftly handling. Nobody seemed to question his qualifications or his right to speak, and as his mistress was beginning to rouse, Loric saw the defeated looks upon the faces of the Guild leaders, whose pathway to easy riches had been resoundingly blocked. Loric did not know it just yet, but in less than two years' time he would find his name submitted for electoral candidacy, and months later he would become the youngest ministerial head in Baystone's history, as he continued his newfound quest to seek fair resolutions, and peaceful diplomacy over violent rule, for the seafaring District of the Empire.

The blur that ensued was only vaguely noticed as Miray was formally awarded the victory and, for the first time in the colosseum’s history, granted her freedom, along with a small parcel of land and servants to aid in adjusting to her new life. name: Patriarch Servid
description:
XVI. With the adrenaline and fear seeping out of him, Brath collapsed to the ground, feeling the toll his body had taken during the combat wash over him. His shoulder aching, Brath struggled to breath deeply, and suspected he would want to visit a healer in Lenha before attempting to make the journey home. Brath dared not attempt to channel any mystic energies, for he knew that he was on his last vestiges of energy, and the nearby area was completely tapped dry... That is, until he saw a large deer step into view across the small glen. Brath stared at the strange creature, baffled by his failing to sense it before it stepped into view, until he heard a voice in his mind.

<We thank you for your assistance, Brath Kindspan, and offer our condolences for not assisting sooner>

Brath paused, before finding his voice.

"Wha-... who are you? What are you doing here? Are you sure you know who I am?" The rush of questions poured out of Brath before he could properly consider decorum while speaking with this clearly-mythical being.

<We are the guardians of these woods, though I'd have figured you would have remembered that. We could not approach that dark creature, lest We allow it to feed on our essences, which would enable it to wreak untold havoc and destruction. Finally, We are familiar with you; do you not remember Us?>

"To be frank, no, I would have thought I'd remember a talking, mythical deer... but my name is Hartwood, not Kindspan."

<Oh? That is peculiar. We were to believe that you inherited the names of your mothers>

Brath's breath caught in his throat.

"My mother...?"

name: Hatuumin Camel
description:
IX. Kontar cradled his bloodied left arm, feeling a wave of relief stave off the impending exhaustion as they stepped towards the small dais. Looking up at Sennie and Sinyu both, Kontar felt secure knowing that they would be able to overcome this, and likely any obstacle, with their combined talents and strength.

Taking a deep breath, and a hand from each of the men, Kontar stepped forward through the portal...

... And into a brilliantly coloured land, adorned with plants and insects, birds and life. Kontar was stunned by the scene, as he had never experienced so many colours, sounds nor seen so many living things in one area for as long as he could remember. After a few moments, Kontar caught his breath, and equally caught himself grinning ear to ear. He turned to look at his companions' expressions... Only to realize that they were not with him. Spinning around, Kontar realized that they were nowhere to be seen, nor was the portal that he had just stepped through.

He was completely, and utterly, alone.
effects:

name: Miray's Signet Ring
description: VIII. Miray saw the looming figure approach, glaring down at her through his visored helm, amusement plain in his eyes as he drew back the trident to deliver the death blow. Miray struggled to get her feet under her, losing her grip on her club in the process, but she could not shake the weight of the net that had anchored itself atop her. Knowing her death was imminent, Miray pushed beyond anything she had previously known, and felt time slow as the trident came bearing down. She glanced to her sides and could see that the weights on the net were loosening from the dirt and realization dawned upon her as she simultaneously grabbed the rear head of her club, knowing what her only hope would be. Abruptly pushing herself to her feet, she felt the prongs of the spear strike down the side of her mail and gouge open her leg as it passed by her, while she shoved the handle of the club up, through the woven netting and into the slit of her opponent’s visor, until she heard a metallic clank.

Presumably owing to the carnage her forces were facing, the Orc Chieftain took the front to challenge the powerful mage who was defending this small hill, and was both shocked-and-amused to find a young, Human girl, alongside a host of creatures, singularly responsible for the mayhem. As per her traditions, the Orc Chieftain challenged Ceta to a duel by combat, on oath of conquest, and Ceta, in her fury, could only nod before setting to the task.

name: Servid Loop
description: VIII.  As Helci Kindspan and Cenius Morgabick's powers collided, the inevitable battle would come to pass. Cenius' thirst for power and destruction had led to his rapid and pronounced growth in his arts, and it was all Helci could do to stave him off. Helci could see that everything she threw at Cenius would have its essence drained and the magicks manevolently returned to her, and it was only when she was at her wits' ends did she realize that the darkest magic, that which she had refused to learn, held the key.... For what was death but the final stage of life?

name: Dromaeon Loop
description: VIII. "Oh, so sorry my friend... it seems you're not quite on your game tod-" The shell-game vendor stopped mid-sentence when he finally raised the shell, and saw the bead still sitting on the table. Helmo smiled to himself, and quickly grabbed the two pouches on the table, tipping his hat until the vendor grabbed his forearm with one arm, brandishing a knife with the other. "Nobody is going to cheat me and walk away, friend!" The vendor growled, but Leoben strummed a quick trio of chords on his lyre before placing a small handful of coins on the table. "And the town guard wouldn't appreciate your game swindling the good people, either, so let bygones be." Leoben stood as the vendor released Helmo's arm, and turned Helmo about as the two began walking away at a brisk pace.

name: Servid Link
description: XV. Feeling the strain in his limbs, Brath continued weaving, attempting to keep brush and foliage between him and Cenius whenever possible. Out of the corner of his eye, Brath saw the unconscious frame of Brianag wilt and sag, undoubtedly having had its life essence drawn out by the reborn apparition. 'So much for those promises of sharing power,' Brath wryly thought to himself. Brath kept moving, heading deeper into the woods and away from the cultists as he began to feel the attacks on his own energies being launched. Brath was well-versed in keeping magical barriers readied at all times - a talent he had unwittingly possessed ever since he was young, though it had apparently manifested in difficulty in both reading and communicating with others - but even with his prolific capabilities he had to strain to keep the beast from drawing his own life force away. Brath continued to evade the shadowed form, hurling the occasional magical counterstroke, until he had gained enough distance from the cultists to make a stand. Drawing energy out of the surrounding life, and feeling numerous plants and small animals perishing under the strain - for which he offered a silent apology - Brath flung the energies of the land, paired with his own, against the shade, watching it recoil and shudder slightly as he did so. The limited reaction was disheartening, but it yielded the effect Brath was hoping for: Cenius attempted to draw upon more power to assail Brath, but there was nothing nearby left to draw on! Brath could feel the creature's shock and panic, and he knew that such a newly-summoned creature would not have sufficient personal energy reserves to draw upon, and at that point it was only a matter of time before it was incapable of even sustaining itself. Brath encapsulated the smoke in a maw of earth - drawn from his own dwindling reserves of energy - before dragging it deep into the dead ground, where it could only dissipate harmlessly.

name: Hatuumin Eye
description:
VIII. Sennie wiped the sweat from his brow as the trio crept down another spiraling stairwell, and the exertion of combat had nearly depleted his magical reserves entirely. Three more times they had been beset by undead creatures, and while he had been able to enchant Sinyu's weapons to be capable of injuring the creatures, it still required a constant flow of magic from him or the enchantment would soon expire.

Sennie was on the verge of recommending they turn back, seeing as Kontar was bloodied and even Sinyu's seeming limitless endurance appeared taxed, when he saw a large pillar encircling a shimmering portal in the chamber before them.

They had made it, somehow... But was the hard part behind them, or before them?

description: You expected these gloves to feel slick, but they move well and seem not leave any residue.

description: Despite these pants being made of tough leathers, they seem capable of quiet, stealthy movements as well.

name: Oiled Leather Boots
description: You've contemplated asking Alanis about which smells worse, the boots or your feet, but one knowing glance, followed by a glare of disapproval, dispelled that notion.

All-Seeing Blindness (Robes): 5% chance to proc 300 Dark damage; +30 damage per Watcher's set item worn

"What, exactly, do you mean to do with that foul-smelling, thick ooze you collected?"

"It's unlikely you'd understand even the most basic principles of mystic research. However, toss me those gloves.... I'd like to try something out."
-Conversations with Alanis

All-Seeing Blindness (Gloves): 5% chance to proc 300 Dark damage; +30 damage per Watcher's set item worn

effects:
All-Seeing Blindness (Pantaloons): 5% chance to proc 300 Dark damage; +30 damage per Watcher's set item worn
Light Armor: +0.2% Evasion

name: Watcher's Boots
description: The oddity of putting on boots made from the skin of a floating, footless creature seems almost comical to you. That being said, you've seen far stranger by now, but it's always nice to appreciate the little things.
effects:
All-Seeing Blindness (Boots): 5% chance to proc 300 Dark damage; +30 damage per Watcher's set item worn
Light Armor: +0.2% Evasion

name: Miray's Bascinet
description: III. The remainder of her practice hour passed uneventfully, and despite the multi-coloured bruises and assorted scars still adorning her frame she felt limber and warmed, her muscles beginning to itch with the anticipation for the bout to come. When the guard arrived to take her to the waiting area she handed him her sword and shield by rote, and she took the light mail chestpiece in return as he gestured for her to follow him. She slipped the mail over her head and fixed the clasps as they walked.
effects: +10 HP; +10 Vitality; +10 Energy

name: Miray's Embrace
description: IV. Securing the final straps as they arrived at the waiting area, the guard nodded respectfully to Miray as he closed the bars to the small room. Miray wordlessly thanked the guard, for he had once again allowed her to retain her helm. Miray could hear the crowds out the opposing exit to the room, noting that even the masses sounded ripe with anticipation. She would typically follow a series of other bouts, which she could follow by the cheers and shouts of the crowds, but today was a special day, with only one publicized battle slated to take place.
effects: +10 HP; +10 Vitality; +10 Energy

name: Miray's Gauntlets
description: V. After a wait that seemed an eternity, Miray heard indistinct calls from the arena, and from the roars of the crowds knew that the battle was imminent even before the guard unlocked and opened the bars that would lead to the arena. Stepping out, Miray squinted at the bright sunlight and the weight of a thousand spectators assembled to witness this match bore down upon her. Only faintly hearing the proclamation of her arrival, she scanned across the stadium and her heart dropped when her eyes focused upon her foe: she could not recognize him under the plate breastpiece, but noted his pale skin tones behind his mail leggings, before returning to his staggering height. This fighter must be at least a head-and-shoulders taller than Miray, who was far-from-slight herself, and they must have been half-again as wide as she!
effects: +10 HP; +10 Vitality; +10 Energy

name: Miray's Legplates
description: VI. Shaking her head, Miray sought to break her train of thought, recalling any number of previous foes who had dwarfed her in stature but fallen all the same. She steadfastly strode forward, watching her opponent do the same as she kept an eye out for the caisson which would hold the arms the two fighters would choose from. As it came into view her heart sank anew, for what typically held a variety of weapons and shields now displayed only four items: a tri-pronged spear with rear-barbed tips and leather grips, a one-handed net with steel weights, a wooden buckler that was obviously incapable of surviving a stabbing strike, and a heavy wooden club. Of course, it would seem that Miray was not intended to emerge from this battle, as the challenger would select his weapons first, leaving Miray with little hope of keeping her pride, let alone her life, intact.
effects: +10 HP; +10 Vitality; +10 Energy

name: Miray's Greaves
description: VII. As they squared off, Miray knew her defeat was close at hand, she vowed on all the Gods that she would not forfeit the match so long as she had breath left in her. Upon hearing the gong signaling the start of the fight her foe was upon her, stabbing forward with the trident with uncanny speed and agility, quickly demonstrating their formal training and expertise with the tool. It was all Miray could do to dodge to the side, taking the butt of the polearm to the side of her helm as she did so. The pair circled, with Miray unable to close the distance but her foe only able to land glancing blows… though as her wounds mounted, Miray knew that her stamina was not infinite, and eventually this dance would end. It was at this stray thought that her foe cast their net into her path, causing her to fall backwards, tangled and helpless.
effects: +10 HP; +10 Vitality; +10 Energy

name: Horned Spangenhelm
description: Adorned equally by the fiercest of women warriors and by worshippers of Derzis, this horned headwear has extra padding on the sides which muffle sounds getting through. While this may be a hindrance when fighting on the battlefield, it will certainly save your ears from devastating claps of thunder.
effects:
Thunderous Fury: 12% chance to proc 120 Lightning damage
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Bassinian Warder Hat
description: The odd design of this hat seems utterly and completely impractical, but Hamond has assured you that he has seen many nobles and aristocrats wearing very similar to some of the larger galas held in the city. The tight felt pressing against your scalp is almost maddening.
effects:
Bassinian Honor (Hat): 7% chance to proc 75 Physical damage; +25 damage per Bassinian Warder set item worn; +15 Energy; +15 Vitality
Light Armor: +0.2% Evasion

name: Bassinian Warder Coat
description: The strange lines on this coat seem almost normal compared to the flowing, draping tails dropping past your knees. Even if the nobility has little reason to recognize functional attire for the prospect of battle, even basic movement, let alone their fancy dances, would be difficult while wearing this garb!
effects:
Bassinian Honor (Coat): 7% chance to proc 75 Physical damage; +25 damage per Bassinian Warder set item worn; +10 Energy; +5 Vitality
Light Armor: +0.2% Evasion

name: Bassinian Warder Gloves
description: Compared to the rest of this outfit you thought the gloves, with a rather simple style to them, were at least relatively normal. After donning them, you realized that the fabric was far too slick to be of any practical use.... but you do have to admit that they feel lovely on your hands.
effects:
Bassinian Honor (Gloves): 7% chance to proc 75 Physical damage; +25 damage per Bassinian Warder set item worn; +5 Energy; +5 Vitality
Light Armor: +0.2% Evasion

name: Bassinian Warder Breeches
description: The threading of these breeches seems to be almost impossibly fine, and while you're accustomed to tight pants as a farmer these certainly push the boundaries of modesty. Maybe the flowing tails of the coat make a little sense, if you have to wear it over these.
effects:
Bassinian Honor (Breeches): 7% chance to proc 75 Physical damage; +25 damage per Bassinian Warder set item worn; +5 Energy; +10 Vitality

However, Edwin has confirmed the quality of the shoes, and seemed to be nigh-besotted with their design, so you've little option but to give them a chance.
effects:
Bassinian Honor (Shoes): 7% chance to proc 75 Physical damage; +25 damage per Bassinian Warder set item worn; +5 Energy; +5 Vitality
Light Armor: +0.2% Evasion

name: Conqueror's Sallet
description: Long ago, this nation was not one country, but dozens of individual ones that frequently quarreled and plotted for power between themselves, often-as-not falling prey to their neighbours while they enacted their own schemes. Temujin, born from humble beginnings, had no grandiose notions of changing that, rather simply wanting to improve the lot of him and his family.
effects:
Temujin's Mercy (Sallet): 4% chance to proc 2000 Physical damage; +800 damage per Conqueror's set item worn; +5% Passive Formation Bonus (Max 1)
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Conqueror's Lamellar Cuirass
description: As a young man, Temujin was nimble but strong, and trained incessantly in whatever homemade crafts he could find, killing his first blood-foe before his sixteenth year. When his village was attacked he rallied the small forces there to route the invaders, slaying many of them and capturing the remainder. When offered the choice to suffer only a few lashes or to serve Temujin, they unwaveringly agreed to follow the young man who had demonstrated such innate skill in battle.
effects:
Temujin's Mercy (Lamellar Cuirass): 4% chance to proc 2000 Physical damage; +800 damage per Conqueror's set item worn; +5% Passive Formation Bonus (Max 1)
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Conqueror's Vambraces
description: Temujin’s early victories were arguably what won him the remainder of his campaign, as his tactical prowess seemed utterly unmatched and undefeatable. His eye for talent was equally gifted, and many of his closest advisors and war-generals were hand-picked from the ranks of his defeated enemies, and they served him faithfully throughout his operations.
effects:
Temujin's Mercy (Vambraces): 4% chance to proc 3000 Physical damage; +800 damage per Conqueror's set item worn; +5% Passive Formation Bonus (Max 1)
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Conqueror's Cuisses
description: Conquering lands one after another, it seemed Temujin could not be stopped, as even his sparse defeats were quickly turned around. In only thirty years Temujin had united over forty lands under his banner, and had taken almost as many lovers in the process.
effects:
Temujin's Mercy (Cuisses): 4% chance to proc 2000 Physical damage; +800 damage per Conqueror's set item worn; +5% Passive Formation Bonus (Max 1)
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Conqueror's Sabatons
description: To this day, it is suggested that better than one-in-twenty of your countrymen share some blood connection with Temujin, though no records remain that would ever substantiate this. The continent mostly existing as a two large countries certainly owes a great debt to Temujin, who unified a swathe of the continent through bloody conquest, in order to bring his desired peace to all peoples he deemed his own.
effects:
Temujin's Mercy (Sabatons): 4% chance to proc 3000 Physical damage; +800 damage per Conqueror's set item worn; +5% Passive Formation Bonus (Max 1)
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

effects:
+2 Energy
Light Armor: +0.2% Evasion

name: Sung Leather Helm
description: III. The first Elves to encounter these staggering, hairy forest people were said to be hostile towards the towering beings, until some of them happened to hear the Songs being Sung, and saw the very beings of the forest bend to the will of the Songs.
effects: Sungwood Symphony (Helm): 15% chance to proc 160 Physical damage; +50 damage per Sung Leather set item worn

name: Sung Leather Vest
description: IV. With all hatred and conflict having fled from their hearts and minds, the Elves tacitly agreed to share their homes, hearths and hearts with the forest people, and a millennia-long harmony was born.
effects: Sungwood Symphony (Vest): 15% chance to proc 160 Physical damage; +50 damage per Sung Leather set item worn

name: Sung Leather Gloves
description: V. During this extended period of peace – at least between the forest people and the Elves – the forest people repaid the kindness shown by the Elves by teaching them how to Sing, breathing life into the inanimate in order to grant it new form and capability.
effects: Sungwood Symphony (Gloves): 15% chance to proc 160 Physical damage; +50 damage per Sung Leather set item worn

name: Sung Leather Pants
description: VI. Millennia later this skill is still passed down amongst the Elves, though the talent to perform the Songs is dwindling at an alarming rate; this has led to Sung wood and Sung leather retaining an astounding value, owing to its rarity.
effects: Sungwood Symphony (Pants): 15% chance to proc 160 Physical damage; +50 damage per Sung Leather set item worn

name: Sung Leather Boots

effects: Sungwood Symphony (Boots): 15% chance to proc 160 Physical damage; +50 damage per Sung Leather set item worn

This lead cleric will attend a gathering, at least once a decade, with the other first disciples, presided over by the 'First Speaker', in order to confirm their circles' deeds and reaffirm their faith in Orimneni.

description: IV. According to Dondomra there has been frequent discord amongst the various circles of Orimneni's followers, however she has refused to provide any details regarding just how far this discord has run. You suspect some of it relates to her willingness to deal with humans, elves and other peoples.
effects:
Granite's Greatness (Chestplate): 15% chance to proc 200 Holy damage; +50 damage per Granite-Blesser's set item worn
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

description: V. You were quite surprised to learn that the orders of Orimneni do not devote themselves specifically to healing, despite their leader being labeled a cleric; Dondomra seemed almost insulted at the suggestion, given how she was garbed in heavy plate-mail and wielded a rod that looked suspiciously like a mace.
effects:
Granite's Greatness (Gauntlets): 15% chance to proc 200 Holy damage; +50 damage per Granite-Blesser's set item worn
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Granite-Blesser's Cuisses
description: VI. Apparently, while Orimneni is the Goddess of the Hearth amongst the dwarves, she is better known as being a defender of Justice, and requires those working in her name to protect the innocent and serve up punishment where necessary. To that end, it is not uncommon for them to work with the Honorbound, for their stiff punishment is as a direct result of dwarven justice being bestowed upon them.
effects:
Granite's Greatness (Cuisses): 15% chance to proc 200 Holy damage; +50 damage per Granite-Blesser's set item worn
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Granite-Blesser's Boots
description: VII. When a follower of Orimneni falls in battle, it is customary to return their body to the hearth of their own home, wrapped in thin cloths and with blessed stones placed upon their eyes and in their hands. Only after this has been accomplished will Orimneni be willing to invite her fallen children to her own hearth for the remainder of eternity.
effects:
Granite's Greatness (Boots): 15% chance to proc 200 Holy damage; +50 damage per Granite-Blesser's set item worn
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

"It's vital you keep debris away from your eyes, including your own hair, human."

"The sounds fair. Could I try yours on, then?"

The Ranger looks at you as though you've grown horns. "Nosatra would disavow me if she caught me bare-headed!"

Scout's Honor (Cap): 12% chance to proc 90 Physical damage; +30 damage per Ranging Scout's set item worn
Light Armor: +0.2% Evasion

description: The key to stealthy movement lies not only in the material of the ranger's clothing, but the appearance as well. The dull, earthen hues of this tunic make for a rather deceptive look when standing amongst foliage.

Scout's Honor (Tunic): 12% chance to proc 90 Physical damage; +30 damage per Ranging Scout's set item worn

"The grip of your gloves is vital, farmer, whether you're holding a bow or wielding a blade. Too firm and you lose versatility, but too slick and you risk your weapon flying from your hand entirely... or so I've heard."

Arendyl colours slightly, and you see Yathane grinning a short distance away.
effects:
Scout's Honor (Gloves): 12% chance to proc 90 Physical damage; +30 damage per Ranging Scout's set item worn
Light Armor: +0.2% Evasion

"Is there any particular reason that these pants are so... tight-fitting?"

The Ranger looks at you sideways. "What good would it do to have baggy pants while climbing trees or venturing through thick woods, farmer?"

"Right, right.... but do they have to be so tight.... all the way up?"

Scout's Honor (Pants): 12% chance to proc 90 Physical damage; +30 damage per Ranging Scout's set item worn

description: A firm foundation requires firm footing, and that can only come from wearing fine boots. The leather of these boots seems unusually light, but the soles seems almost impossibly firm, without sacrificing flexibility. It's no wonder the scouts can move so silently, with boots like these!
effects:
Scout's Honor (Boots): 12% chance to proc 90 Physical damage; +30 damage per Ranging Scout's set item worn
Light Armor: +0.2% Evasion

name: Honorbound's Veil
description:

name: Embersin Plate
description: II. Legend describes Saijeor's first slaying of a dragon when he was still a boy, cutting the throat of a wicked youngling that had forayed into his village and had already slain several men in its hunger. Some of the tales describe the battle as fierce, with Saijeor suffering permanent, scarring injuries, while others claim he suffered not a scratch. In either case, Saijeor's name and reputation quickly spread after the event.
effects:
+6% Fire Resistance; +20 HP
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Embersin Gauntlets
description: III. Several years later, Saijeor attended a neighbouring kingdom, after the king had published a reward on the life of a particularly malevolent dragon who, as they were known to do in those days, demanded sacrifices in order to avoid drowning the kingdom in fire. After the drake had demanded the life of the king's only daughter, Saijeor attended to accept the request for aid. Unlike the dozens of champions who had gone before him, Saijeor did not immediately fall to the dragon's firey wrath and was able to emerge victorious, winning the hearts of the citizens and the hand of the princess in marriage.
effects:
+6% Fire Resistance; +20 HP

Those describing his ongoing crusade against the dragons detail a variety of both heroic and dishonourable acts, ranging from saving villagers from being attacked, to locating dragon nests to destroy their brood before they even hatched.

Despite her father strictly adhering to tradition in virtually every other aspect of his life, he could never turn down a heartfelt request from his daughter, and when she declared that she intended to take up the blade and train as the other samurai students would.

Despite Nakano being initially unarmed, she was able to fend off the attackers, disarming them while arming herself, maiming one and killing another before the third fled.
