Nakano was even willing to forego her inheritance of the estate, despite being the eldest child, in order to continue her mastery of the blade. To that end she took steps to ensure the safety of her younger brother and her house, as rumours had begun spreading of a thief who could decimate a house's fortunes in a single night, if they did not slaughter all the occupants out of hand.

As she passed by the door to her brother's rooms, however, she caught a glimpse of a shadow, and instinctively rushed to action.

description: II. The woods offered a terrific escape, and after years of spending virtually all her free moments there she knew them better than she knew the scars on the backs of her hands. She could identify every tree and bush, not only by species but even individually from each other of its kind, and she was intimately familiar with the fauna of the forest as well.

Tessa's father was a sinewy man, and not well adapted to the wilderness, had been unable to teach Tessa how to use a bow and arrow when she asked at a young age, so after she found a discarded crossbow she had to teach herself how to shoot on that style of weapon. While it was certainly slower and had less range than a conventional bow, Tessa took an immediate liking to the tool and honed her skills over the next few years.

When Tessa was in her 16th year, Volevar fell into considerable hardship when a large gathering of bandits took to the Dartpar Woods and began waylaying every merchant or supply cart that attempted to make it into or out of the small town. After two months of no new supplies making their ways into the village, several groups of villagers attempted to chase off the bandits, but only returned in pieces.

Set Bonus (Fogcreek Set) for wearing the stated number of equipment pieces from this set:

Tessa, seething with anger at how terribly these new occupants treated her precious woods - her true home, as she considered it - was unable to sit idle, and snuck out of the village late one night. No-one knows for certain how she did it, but over the following week bandits were being downed by the dozen, and in under ten days the entire group disbanded and fled.

name: Mephitic Chiffon Cap
description:
X. Recognizing the urgency of the situation, Brath knew he would have to forgo watching and take action, and he did so by weaving a powerful spell of hypnotic visions. Wordlessly thanking the natural elements that were donating the last of their essences for this action - and their sacrifices would not be in vain! - Brath directed it at the four cultists he saw before him, attempting to lull them into a daze such that they could be bound without sounding any alarm. Somewhat to Brath's surprise the spell worked with an unseemly effectiveness, causing all three to stop their chanting and instead produce a drawled groaning sound, but of a similar pitch to the chant. Brath rushed forward and grabbed each, one at a time, dragging them back to the brush and ensnaring each in roots, before stepping out to search for his next target.

"Well, that went more smoothly than expected, didn't it?"

Brath was startled as he heard a feminine voice from an unseen source, and realized that came from above him. Cursing his luck and bounding forward, Brath took note that the tree behind him was already dead, which is presumably why he had been unable to detect the presence of anyone within it. As he moved into the open, however, Brath felt hands grab at his arms and head, forcing him down and driving his shoulder into the ground.
effects:
Downy Destruction (Cap): 40% chance to proc 375 Holy damage; +6250 damage vs Guild Raids; +125 damage per Mephitic Chiffon set item worn; 25% chance to proc 400 Poison damage; +150 damage per Mephitic Chiffon set item worn
Set Bonus (Mephitic Chiffon Set) for wearing the stated number of equipment pieces from this set:
 9+: +6% All Resistances; 1% chance to heal the Player 5 HP
Light Armor: +0.2% Evasion

name: Mephitic Chiffon Fur
description:
XI. "Well, it seems the bait was finally taken," the woman's voice said overtop of Brath, as he was pulled up to his feet and his hands bound. The cowled figure lifted her hood, revealing a young woman's face adorned with a series of scars. "And it seems we were fortunate enough to bag one that had some talent for the arts, this time! This, my sisters and brothers, may be the final piece we need to call forth our namesake and partake of his power for our own!"

Brath was led through the brush to a small stone altar, somewhat bewildered until he heard several utterances of 'For Cenius' and 'Hail Ceniuspact!' as he passed.

Brath was startled when the apparent leader, whom he assumed to be Brianag, sliced open his palm with a small dagger, flicking the blood into the pyre before the altar before licking the other side of the blade.

"By Morgabick's wrath, how could this be? You aren't old enough to be them... and yet, it must be true! You had a hand in the thwarting of Cenius himself?! How could he fall to such a weakling as yourself!" Brianag uttered, and several of the cultists fell to their knees, gazing up in awe.

"No matter... this only works to our benefit, for you will certainly be the last sacrifice we require in order to bring forth our Dark Master's deepest powers!"
effects:
Downy Destruction (Fur): 40% chance to proc 375 Holy damage; +6250 damage vs Guild Raids; +125 damage per Mephitic Chiffon set item worn; 25% chance to proc 400 Poison damage; +150 damage per Mephitic Chiffon set item worn

name: Mephitic Chiffon Gloves
description:
XII. Brath watched wordlessly as fuel was added to the pyre before him, and the cultists congregated in the area in their final preparations. He could see bloodstains on the stone altar, feeling his hand sting as his own blood slowly dripped from it, and recognized a number of the other reagents visible around the fire, and could guess how they were using them to forcibly draw out the land's natural energy.

"What are you hoping to gain from all of this?" Brath asked one of the cultists as they drew close. Brianag wheeled around, seemingly dead-set upon being the one to respond.

"The Ceniuspact will gain and share the powers of the great Cenius, you dullard. I would have expected that much to be obvious, even to a layperson."

"And how is it you plan to harness and control those powers? You seem to be lacking a binding agent of any potency, at a glance..."

"Ah, so you aren't the dullard you let on, it seems. Cenius himself, transcending death to speak with me in visions, has provided me detailed instructions, and with the ongoing ritual magics we will be able to partake of his power willingly in gratitude for restoring the faith!"

Sighing in resignment, Brath uttered a short incantation - and taking care to draw upon his own energies rather than any fragile plant-life - loosening his bindings and standing up quickly, stretching his bruised shoulder as he rose.
effects:
Downy Destruction (Gloves): 40% chance to proc 375 Holy damage; +6250 damage vs Guild Raids; +125 damage per Mephitic Chiffon set item worn; 25% chance to proc 400 Poison damage; +150 damage per Mephitic Chiffon set item worn

name: Mephitic Chiffon Pants
description:
XIII. Brianag's confusion was palpable as Brath watched her face before unleashing a bolt of energy to propel her backwards. Brath didn't know how Cenius had communicated with her, or what exactly he had conveyed to her, but he had no intention of letting her see his plans through, and Brath quickly kneeled down to place both of his hands upon the ground. Gritting his teeth with the effort - Brath was still keenly aware that the energy required for his spells would have to come from his own reserves, given the strain the cult had placed upon the area with their rituals - Brath began chanting quickly, causing the ground to ebb upwards, before heaving down, starting a violent tremor that violently shook the area. The cultists that had still be standing were knocked prone, several of which suffering violent injury as they fell, and several of the nearby trees began to topple as the spell took effect. Offering a silent prayer for their sacrifice, Brath called forth the last reserves of those fallen trees - whose lives would otherwise pass needlessly - and summoned stone bindings for the cultists on the ground, sealing them tight to the earth.

"This is impossible! You were our sacrifice! He was going to share his power!" Brianag screamed in futility as she was bound against a tree opposite Brath, and he could only shrug... Until the pyre flared out entirely and the area went dark. Brath looked back at the remnants of the fire, then heard a series of moans; when Brath looked back, he could see a dark energy flowing out of Brianag's mouth as her back arched unnaturally, and then he could see similar from the other cultists. The energy all approached and coalesced over the fire, and Brath's heart sank when he saw a face in the dark cloud, one that he recognized from his nightmares. Cenius, in some form, appeared to have returned.

Downy Destruction (Pants): 40% chance to proc 375 Holy damage; +6250 damage vs Guild Raids; +125 damage per Mephitic Chiffon set item worn; 25% chance to proc 400 Poison damage; +150 damage per Mephitic Chiffon set item worn

name: Mephitic Chiffon Hooves
description:
XIV. Before the shock of what had transpired had passed, Brath was besieged with a torrent of dark energy. The apparition cackled in an unearthly tone as it flung spell after spell at him. Brath nimbly dodged several blasts of energy, rebuffing several others with his own magic, and winced while he felt the last vestiges of energy drain from the nearby land as Cenius drew out their life essence with ease. Recognizing that he was not going to win by simply dodging, Brath drew up a spear of earth from below the smoky form, but as it pierced upwards it met with no resistance, and did nothing to stifle the cackling laughter from the apparition. Brath dodged another series of energy waves before being slammed forward by a vine, knocking him off balance. As he stumbled, Brath noticed the face of one of the cultists he had ensnared, appearing utterly dehydrated and mummified.

'Of course,' Brath thought to himself. 'He is drawing the life energy out of the cultists themselves!'

name: Mordant Moswa Antlers
description: III. Brath rubbed his temples as he tried to nurse away a pending headache, and days' worth of fatigue from poor sleep. He was used to sleeping soundly, but in recent weeks his dreams had been haunted by strange visions and voices, of a visage that seemed fleetingly familiar drawing out the essence of the land, and flashings of an ethereal, skeletal hand cloaked in an endlessly dark shroud uttering words that, to the best he could figure, sounded of Naog Loga Rogag Dez Zoz Naog Arokak. The soothing motion of his fingers on his tired temples, coupled with the late-afternoon sun baring down on him in his chair, caused Brath's breathing to draw deeper, and in moments he was out... Until he saw the visions anew, coupled with a panic and fear that he had previously never experienced, and the clear utterance of a single word: Cenius.

description:
IV. Jolting awake, Brath Hartwood reeled forward at the recollection of this latest nightmare, realizing his face was covered in perspiration only as it began to drip onto the documents clumsily adorning his desk. He knew that a typical dream would never present him with new information, but the term 'Cenius' was clearly new to him. Brath rushed out of his small office and past the acolytes studying - well, most of them were goofing off more than studying - in the lounge, and headed for the Academy's senior library.

After several hours of review, Brath was left aghast and awestruck. The name Cenius was reported to have belonged to a dark Druid not 40 years past, whose rise to power was swift and terrible, but who disappeared without a trace for no apparent reason. The strange phrase he had heard, as best he could translate from some ancient Gak runes, translated to something akin to 'The void will be coming for you - or perhaps because of you -,' and 'your part is not done.' Brath didn't know what to make of that, but returned his attention to the Cenius character.
effects:
Acidic Antlers (Fur): 25% chance to proc 2080 Magic damage; +10000 damage vs Guild Raids; +320 damage per Mordant Moswa set item worn; 6% chance to proc 6000 Nature damage; +1000 damage per Mordant Moswa set item worn; 25% chance to proc 400 Acid damage; +150 damage per Mordant Moswa set item worn
Light Armor: +0.2% Evasion

name: Mordant Moswa Gloves
description: V. The only references Brath could find regarding the name, at least in any meaningful academic sense, spoke of Cenius the Lifeswindler, but little was written about his end - or if he had even truly passed on. This Cenius character was apparently masterfully adept at drawing out life essence, even from utterly unwilling victims, and turning those energies back upon their original hosts and others. It seems some individuals had attempted to replicate that power, but none had proven anywhere near as successful as Cenius, himself, had been. There was a name that came up several times, however, which troubled Brath: Brianag. He was certain he had heard that name referenced before, but he could not quite remember from where. Scribbling fiercely, Brath returned his books, and only upon his turning to depart did the kindly librarian speak up.

Light Armor: +0.2% Evasion

name: Mordant Moswa Pants
description:
VI. "Oh, you're looking into those recent disappearances, then?" Brath was utterly taken aback, both by the librarian - who had previously always remained silent in his presence - speaking up, and by the comment itself. 

"What do you mean? What disappearances?"

"Oh, I just assumed from that paper with the name Brianag, and given all the trouble surrounding her in recent months... My apologies if I disturbed your thoughts, professor."

Brath paused, before rushing over to the section housing the recent articles surrounding world events. It took some digging, but he was able to find several different sources citing issues in the Voysa Province, including a number of disappearances of semi-prominent figures, and a following seeming to arise around this Brianag individual. Brath could find little information detailing her history, beyond mention that she had been a budding scholar of the arcane, but this new information planted a seed of deep fear into Brath's mind: Brianag must be amassing a cult of followers for dark purposes.
effects:
Acidic Antlers (Pants): 25% chance to proc 2080 Magic damage; +10000 damage vs Guild Raids; +320 damage per Mordant Moswa set item worn; 6% chance to proc 6000 Nature damage; +1000 damage per Mordant Moswa set item worn; 25% chance to proc 400 Acid damage; +150 damage per Mordant Moswa set item worn
Light Armor: +0.2% Evasion

name: Mordant Moswa Hooves
description:
VII. Brath hastily made arrangements with the Headmaster to take a hopefully-brief sabbatical to the Voysa Province before planning his next steps. The majority of the recent events he had been able to find suggested issues in the capital of Rispa, but Brath had suspicions that the heart of the issue lay elsewhere... Though whether it was the Celelfen Swamp, the Vermond Mountains or the Lenha Woods was unclear. Brath decided to depart for Rispa and see what he could find there, as the new information would at least be fresh.

The journey was uneventful, though it did take him the better part of a month by wagon, and Brath could only hope that there nothing else untoward had transpired in that time. After arriving at the city, Brath decided to visit a recommended inn to gather his wits and prepare for the search the following day, and bedded early that eve. His sleep was haunted, however, by the visions that had befallen him while still at the Academy, only seemingly starker in contrast and far more vivid. Overlaid over the name Cenius, which always followed after the spectral, cloaked hands reached out for him, Brath saw a hooded shape, and a young woman pulled it back to reveal a scarred face while she stood before a fire in a dark forest. Brath woke in a panic, covered in perspiration, and barely slept a wink the remainder of the night.

name: Hatuumin Headdress
description:
III. Sinyu was sweating in the daytime heat as he took a break from training the newest batch of recruits, and he was surprised to see Kontar running up to him. Sinyu's heart panged him whenever he saw the youth, and he wondered how his own child, and wife, were doing back in Agiz, but his new work training up the private militia groups, both physically and in tactics, kept him occupied.

Kontar was winded but brief: He had need to speak with Sinyu, and would do so this evening at a location they both knew. Sinyu sighed, suspecting he'd have to turn the boy down once again, but agreeing to meet up with him prior to returning to his duties.

name: Hatuumin Armor
description:
IV. Sinyu was unsurprised to see Kontar waiting for him, near the crumbling remains of the old storehouse, but was surprised to see a young mage slowly step out behind him. Sinyu grimaced; he had little move the mages, even during his time as a battle commander, but he heard Kontar out.

As he listened, Sinyu's mind turned at the tale, which involved this Sennie fellow making use of a cursed book (yet another fault of foolish mages), and needing to seek absolution by... entering the land of the dead?!

Sinyu was about to immediately turn down an invitation for such a quest, until Kontar relayed that the secret entrance to this portal was in a tomb near to Agiz. Unsure how Kontar would know such a thing, the only thing Sinyu could think of was that this was his opportunity to finally go home.
effects:
Sight of the Eye (Armor): 8% chance to proc 825 Psychic damage; +225 damage per Hatuumin set item worn; 5% chance to reduce incoming damage to the Player by 5%
Set Bonus (Hatuumin Set) for wearing the stated number of equipment pieces from this set:
 9+: Additional Bonuses depending on Main-Hand/Off-Hand equipped

name: Hatuumin Gloves
description: V. Two mornings later, after making preparations for the journey, the trio departed Yubqal, heading south towards Agiz, and hoping to keep to the main trail as best as they could manage. Sinyu kept a wary eye upon Sennie, uncertain of this new companion's true intentions and disliking how he seemed to be rubbing his palms and wrists, but he trusted Kontar's intentions, and if Kontar agreed with this plan then he could hardly balk.
effects:
Sight of the Eye (Gloves): 8% chance to proc 825 Psychic damage; +225 damage per Hatuumin set item worn; 5% chance to reduce incoming damage to the Player by 5%
Set Bonus (Hatuumin Set) for wearing the stated number of equipment pieces from this set:
 9+: Additional Bonuses depending on Main-Hand/Off-Hand equipped

name: Hatuumin Dressings
description: VI. While the first day of travel was uneventful, the first night was not, as they were assailed by bandits under the veil of darkness. Sennie whipped up a fury, wielding his trusted whips and mace, but it wasn't long before he realized there were simply too many, and they would be overrun... That was, however, until he was ready to face the bite of a bandit's glaive, only to watch the bandit's eyes roll back and blood pour out, and only then realized that Sennie had struck him down. The trio staved off the bandits, Kontar wielding his darts and Sennie his magics, and Sinyu breathed a sigh of relief, knowing he'd live to fight another day.
effects:
Sight of the Eye (Dressings): 8% chance to proc 825 Psychic damage; +225 damage per Hatuumin set item worn; 5% chance to reduce incoming damage to the Player by 5%
Set Bonus (Hatuumin Set) for wearing the stated number of equipment pieces from this set:
 9+: Additional Bonuses depending on Main-Hand/Off-Hand equipped

name: Hatuumin Boots
description: VII. After that initial night, the remaining weeks of travel were banal, with the days scorching and the nights freezing, but Sinyu started recognizing the terrain as they grew closer to Agiz. Kontar took them off the path, towards a rocky outcropping, and after shifting some small stones he revealed a hidden switch that opened a small portal, with stairs leading downwards. Sinyu's amazement was cut short as two eyeless, bandage-wrapped beasts leaped out of the opening, and while his blows seemed capable of pushing them back, they were unable to damage the beasts. Luckily Sennie stepped in with his magic and was capable of dispatching the creatures, though not without exhaustive effort.

name: Rusted Leoegan Helmet
description: I. The Thiadoran tales of the various demons and villains plaguing their ancient lands are numerous and varied, though many seem to agree that the origination of the demons in the lands was the fault of Paython.
effects:
Rusty Jawlocking (Helmet): 5% chance to proc 400 Physical damage
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Rusted Leoegan Linothorax
description: II. Like many tales of old, it is not uncommon for it to begin with one of the Gods straying from their righteous seats and choosing a mortal favourite. Vozotl was no exception to this, in many respects, and it is said that Paython was born of that union.
effects:
Rusty Jawlocking (Linothorax): 5% chance to proc 400 Physical damage
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Rusted Leoegan Gloves
description: III. Paython's upbringing was apparently quite typical, as the headstrong boy was as ready to scrap as he was to learn, but his mother cared deeply for him. The lingering absence of his father was a persisting thorn in the boy's side, however.
effects:
Rusty Jawlocking (Gloves): 5% chance to proc 400 Physical damage
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Rusted Leoegan Greaves
description: IV. Despite many years of vagueries, by the time Paython was near-manhood his mother relented and shared the truth of his parentage with him. Paython was overjoyed, until he realized that he had no way of confirming the tale to those who had previously mocked him.
effects:
Rusty Jawlocking (Greaves): 5% chance to proc 400 Physical damage
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Rusted Leoegan Boots
description: V. Paython knew that he had to find some way of confirming his heritage, such that it could be displayed to the world to showcase his greatness. His resolve stoked, he set off for the great Temple of Vozotl in what-would-eventually be Gipanta.
effects:
Rusty Jawlocking (Boots): 5% chance to proc 400 Physical damage
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Leoegan Helmet
description: VI. After several days of travel, Paython rushed to the great Temple, only to be turned away by Vozotl's adherents. Someone as unclean, unkempt and uncouth as this youth was unsuitable and unready to give praise to Vozotl's greatness.
effects:
By the Gods (Helmet): 6% chance to proc 500 Physical damage
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Leoegan Linothorax
description: VII. Paython took these insults to heart, and after realizing he had no means to support himself took to the streets. He spent several days begging before he was able to amass sufficient funds for a proper inn, where he could bathe and clean himself and return to the Temple a humbled - and better smelling - visitor.
effects:
By the Gods (Linothorax): 6% chance to proc 500 Physical damage
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Leoegan Gloves
description: VIII. Baring himself before the altar of Vozotl, Paython pleaded for answers to why he was abandoned, why he had no proof of his parentage and, perhaps most importantly, whether his father even loved him. All he received in reply was silence.
effects:
By the Gods (Gloves): 6% chance to proc 500 Physical damage
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Leoegan Greaves
description: IX. Disheartened, Paython left the temple, bitter in his being scorned by the Gods, and questioning his worth when his father still refused to even acknowledge his existence.
effects:
By the Gods (Greaves): 6% chance to proc 500 Physical damage
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Leoegan Boots
description: X. As he was descending the hill the temple rested upon, Paython was knocked to his feet when an unexpected blast of lightning struck down from the skies, setting some nearby brush alight. As the ringing in Paython's ears started to die down, he saw the flames dance in a strange fashion, before surging forward on the ground towards him. They burned hot, before winking out, leaving strange scorch marks in the earth.
effects:
By the Gods (Boots): 6% chance to proc 500 Physical damage
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Sanctified Leoegan Helmet
description: XII. Arriving at the smaller temple, Paython as greeted by several acolytes who informed him that their worship was devoted to Derzis. Passing a thin figure with strange, electrical protrusions, Paython made his way to the altar of Derzis, and fell to his knees. Without warning a flash of lightning struck the altar, leaving Paython seeing only whiteness as every hair on his body stood erect.
effects:
As the Gods Command (Helmet): 8% chance to proc 350 Holy damage; +100 damage per Sanctified Leoegan set item worn
Sanctified
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Sanctified Leoegan Linothorax
description: XIII. Paython heard a voice, though he could not see the source - or much of anything else - as it commiserated with him. "My half-brother does not deign to interfere, even when it is clear he should. Oh, he has his dalliances, to be sure, but where he is particular about the consequences of his worshippor's actions he thinks little of his own."
effects:
As the Gods Command (Linothorax): 8% chance to proc 350 Holy damage; +100 damage per Sanctified Leoegan set item worn
Sanctified
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Sanctified Leoegan Gloves
description: XIV. "If you wish his attention, it may be better to seek it from his mother, Amera. She was also able to make him jump on command, so that is my advice to you. I wish you well on this quest, young mortal." Paython was stunned as his vision began to clear, and he saw no-one else sharing the chamber with him.
effects:
As the Gods Command (Gloves): 8% chance to proc 350 Holy damage; +100 damage per Sanctified Leoegan set item worn
Sanctified
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Sanctified Leoegan Greaves
description: XV. With the sun beginning to set, Paython hurriedly thanked the acolytes in the temple before taking off, seeking out the temple of Amera. Unsurprisingly, Her temple was the largest in the city, overlooking a cliff's edge and out onto the ocean. Paython hastily made his way to the temple just as the sun was on the verge of winking out behind the mountains, only to be met with barred doors.
effects:
As the Gods Command (Greaves): 8% chance to proc 350 Holy damage; +100 damage per Sanctified Leoegan set item worn
Sanctified
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Sanctified Leoegan Boots
description: XVI. Paython was disappointed to hear that parishioners of Amera's temple could only pay their respects during the day, as it was Irus who ruled come nightfall, allowing Amera to rest. As the sun disappeared behind the mountains, Paython's heart was heavy and his thoughts scattered, so he started towards the cliff's edge to compose himself, when something caught his eye.
effects:
As the Gods Command (Boots): 8% chance to proc 350 Holy damage; +100 damage per Sanctified Leoegan set item worn
Sanctified
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Consecrated Leoegan Helmet
description: XX. With a quick motion Paython directed the flaming steeds ahead, and he realized his folley as they rushed towards the edge of the cliff. Fearing he had doomed himself, and his mission, on an unplanned whim, Paython was struck by wonder when the chariot, rather than plummeting towards the rocky waters, continued forward and upwards, taking off into the sky. Jubilant and filled with wonder, he urged the stallions onward, and they flew up at a steep trajectory.
effects:
At the Pleasure of the Gods (Helmet): 10% chance to proc 400 Holy damage; +200 damage per Consecrated Leoegan set item worn; 2% chance to proc 1,500 Holy damage
Consecrated
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion
effectName: At the Pleasure of the Gods (Helmet)

name: Consecrated Leoegan Linothorax
description: XXI. Before long, however, Paython was feeling a deep chill, and found himself gasping for air. Looking around, even the great city was but a speck below him, but Paython knew he had to descend. Twisting on the reins to turn the steeds, the stallions turned back to the west, before diving down sharply.
effects:
At the Pleasure of the Gods (Linothorax): 10% chance to proc 400 Holy damage; +200 damage per Consecrated Leoegan set item worn; 2% chance to proc 1,500 Holy damage
Consecrated
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion
effectName: At the Pleasure of the Gods (Linothorax)

name: Consecrated Leoegan Gloves
description: XXII. As the ground rapidly approached, the stallions leveled out, but Paython realized something was amiss. He looked behind him, and was nearly blinded by a flaming sphere that was chasing after the chariot. 'Of course,' he thought to himself, 'this chariot pulls the sun... But this is far too low for such a power to be. What have I done?!' Paython could only bemoan the deep scars he was carving in the earth, leaving molten ruin in his wake.
effects:
At the Pleasure of the Gods (Gloves): 10% chance to proc 400 Holy damage; +200 damage per Consecrated Leoegan set item worn; 2% chance to proc 1,500 Holy damage
Consecrated
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion
effectName: At the Pleasure of the Gods (Gloves)

name: Consecrated Leoegan Greaves
description: XXIII. Unable to drive the chariot back to the sky, Paython was flooded with regret as he realized the devastation he was causing, and knew there was nothing more he could do to end the terror. Of course, just because there was nothing more that he could do did not mean intervention was impossible... In an inexplicable act of divine intervention, a ray of fire, so hot it almost seemed to be liquid, struck the chariot, leaving only a black mark where Paython had previously stood.
effects:
At the Pleasure of the Gods (Greaves): 10% chance to proc 400 Holy damage; +200 damage per Consecrated Leoegan set item worn; 2% chance to proc 1,500 Holy damage
Consecrated
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion
effectName: At the Pleasure of the Gods (Greaves)

name: Consecrated Leoegan Boots
description: XXIV. As the stories go, Amera's steeds were able to find their way back to Her temple without any further issue, but the damage to the land was already done. Whether the land had been burned down to the Hells, or Paython had merely unearthed some trapped, subterranean denizens, demons spilled forth into the land from the maars, ushering in an age of despair and horrors for both the Thiadorans and Acherallans.
effects:
At the Pleasure of the Gods (Boots): 10% chance to proc 400 Holy damage; +200 damage per Consecrated Leoegan set item worn; 2% chance to proc 1,500 Holy damage
Consecrated
Heavy Armor: -2% Evasion

name: Quilltip's Cap
description: I. The life of an apprentice was rarely one of greatness, accolades or riches, but the life of a scribe... well, that was even less so. At least the apprentice had the opportunity to learn the master's skills and expand upon their own. Loric Quilltip had thought these depressing thoughts a dozen times over in the past few days, ever since being promoted from the lowly role of assistant to helping out with his new master's campaign. Worst of all, he was stuck in the presence of politicians, day in and day out.
effects:
Set Bonus (Quilltip's Set) for wearing the stated number of equipment pieces from this set:
 5+: +40 Magic and +1 Tiers of 'Attentive Perception'

name: Quilltip's Shirt
description: II. "Be sure that the mason's guild gets their four percent commission. Scratch that, offer three percent, and if they balk we can counter with three and a half. Are you getting all of that?" Loric refrained from sighing for the dozenth time this morning, merely nodding and murmuring an 'mmhmm' to acknowledge his employer. Loric had already learned that she did not want him to actually speak, but she hated being ignored even more, so this seemed to be the best compromise to avoid another lashing. Loric scribbled furiously on his parchment, and continued following the young noblewoman down the lengthy hallway, taking down what he could while, unavoidably, taking in more than he wished.
effects:
Set Bonus (Quilltip's Set) for wearing the stated number of equipment pieces from this set:
 5+: +40 Magic and +1 Tiers of 'Attentive Perception'

name: Quilltip's Gloves
description: III. Loric's general distaste for his new position only grew over the following weeks, and while he was passed over to work with his mistress's colleague for a period - during which he realized that there were significantly worse people to work for than his mistress - he knew he had limited options if he wished to continue living with a roof over his head, and to help cover the costs his ailing mother was racking up.
effects:
Set Bonus (Quilltip's Set) for wearing the stated number of equipment pieces from this set:
 5+: +40 Magic and +1 Tiers of 'Attentive Perception'

name: Quilltip's Pants
description: IV. By his fourth month working for his mistress Loric had come to recognize her mannerisms and patterns of speech, and he merely assumed it had come as a natural part of the job. Loric found himself scribbling what his mistress was going to say moments before she said it, and was even able to provide the occasional word or phrase when she lost her train of thought, and had no real fear of receiving a strike upon his thighs for the input. This did little to reduce the stress his mistress was under, considering a number of strikes had broken out in Baystone, and the entire District of Sebrook seemed ready to revolt against their elected officials if something didn't change. Loric watched as his mistress' skin became paler and took silent notice of the meals she was missing as time went on.
effects:
Set Bonus (Quilltip's Set) for wearing the stated number of equipment pieces from this set:
 5+: +40 Magic and +1 Tiers of 'Attentive Perception'

name: Quilltip's Shoes
description:
V. Not a week later, Loric was raising his hand to shade his eyes as the sun beat down on him during a ministerial address his mistress was scheduled to make. The heads of several of the District's largest Guilds were present and awaiting a speech by the official, and were expected to make their own proclamations in response, which would set the stage for either quelling the fires of revolt, or setting ablaze the pyres of revolution. As his ashen-skinned mistress stepped forward to speak, she wobbled slightly, before catching herself and taking a deep breath. It seemed not to help, however, as by her third word she collapsed to the stone ground in a heap. Panic and commotion broke out in the crowd, and Loric saw the leaders of both the Forgemasters' and Masons' Guilds stepping forward, greed painted on their faces as they sought to seize an opportunity.

"What my lady was trying to say is that, after careful consultation with the officials in both Tahyrst and Haleah, we are prepared to meet with the Guild Chapters of each of these Districts in order to ensure an equitable redistribution of contracts. While it is undeniable that work relating to the shipyards has led to disproportionate compensation, the same cannot be said of other fields, such as forging."

Loric was unsure what had come over him, and had no recollection of when he had stepped up to the podium, but he took a breath before continuing.

"It is long past time that the Guild Chapters remain fragmented while being expected to work under the same contract conditions, and only by a willingness to bring everyone to the table for reasonable discussions and bargaining can positive change be effected."
effects:
Set Bonus (Quilltip's Set) for wearing the stated number of equipment pieces from this set:
 5+: +40 Magic and +1 Tiers of 'Attentive Perception'
